meta.title: Integration Interfaces — Building System Interconnections
meta.description: All fire alarm system integration points with other building systems

interfaces.name: Elevator Recall (Phase 1 & Phase 2)
trigger_devices: Smoke detector in each elevator lobby
Smoke detector in elevator machine room
Heat detector in elevator machine room

phase1_recall.description: All elevators recalled to designated recall floor (typically ground floor)
phase1_recall.action: Elevators travel to recall floor, open doors, and go out of service

inputs_to_panel.signal: Alarm → initiate Phase 1 recall for elevators serving Floor X
Alarm → initiate Phase 1 recall for ALL elevators

outputs_from_panel.signal: Dry contact (NO) — closes on recall command
outputs_from_panel.quantity: 1 per elevator group or per car

phase2_recall.description: Firefighter's Phase 2 — if smoke in hoistway, recall to alternate floor
phase2_recall.action: Elevators recalled to ALTERNATE designated floor
phase2_recall.notes: Only activated when Phase 1 has already been triggered

monitoring_points.description: Fire alarm panel must monitor elevator status

interfaces.standards: NFPA 72 Section 21.6 / ASME A17.1 Chapter 2 / BS 5839-1 Section 21.3 / BS EN 81-72
interfaces.notes: Elevator recall is one of the most critical fire alarm integrations — must be tested during commissioning with elevator contractor present

interfaces.name: HVAC Shutdown / Smoke Control
trigger_devices: Zone smoke detectors (if smoke control required)

fan_shutdown.description: Shut down air handling unit serving affected zone
fan_shutdown.action: Energize relay to interrupt power to AHU motor starter
fan_shutdown.signal: Dry contact from fire alarm control relay module

damper_closure.description: Close fire/smoke dampers in affected zone
damper_closure.action: Release actuator power (fail-close) or energize close signal
damper_closure.type: Fire dampers (fusible link — close at 165°F) OR smoke dampers (motorized — controlled by fire alarm)

smoke_control.description: Activate smoke exhaust and pressurization fans
smoke_control.action: Sequence of operations to manage smoke movement
smoke_control.complexity: May require dedicated smoke control panel with multiple stages

duct_detector_locations.supply_air: Downstream of filters, before any branch or coil
duct_detector_locations.return_air: Before exhaust connection, after recirculation connection
duct_detector_locations.access: Within 3 ft (1m) of duct detector housing
duct_detector_locations.required_when: HVAC system > 2000 CFM serving more than one zone (NFPA) or per risk assessment (BS)

interfaces.standards: NFPA 72 Section 21.8 / BS 5839-1 Section 21.2 / IMC Section 607 / NFPA 92
interfaces.notes: HVAC shutdown prevents smoke distribution — one of the most important life safety integrations

interfaces.name: Fire Door Release / Magnetic Hold-Open
interfaces.trigger: Any fire alarm signal OR smoke detector in adjacent area

magnetic_holder_release.description: Remove power from electromagnetic door holders to allow fire doors to close
magnetic_holder_release.action: Fail-safe — doors close when power is removed
magnetic_holder_release.power: 24VDC from fire alarm panel (or separate power with fire alarm interface)
magnetic_holder_release.devices_per_circuit: Limited by current draw — use relay modules for multiple holders

monitoring.description: Fire alarm panel should monitor door holder status
monitoring.notes: If door holder loses power unexpectedly → trouble signal

interfaces.standards: NFPA 72 Section 21.9 / NFPA 80 / BS 5839-1 Section 21.1 / BS 7273-4
interfaces.notes: Critical for fire compartmentation — ensure ALL fire doors are on hold-open release system

interfaces.name: Sprinkler System Monitoring

water_flow_switch.description: Detects water flowing through sprinkler system — indicates activated sprinkler head(s)
water_flow_switch.location: On main sprinkler riser (one per system/zone)
water_flow_switch.notes: Water flow = fire condition — highest priority alarm

control_valve_tamper.description: Detects when sprinkler control valve is partially or fully closed (system impaired)
control_valve_tamper.location: On each sprinkler control valve (OS&Y or PIV)
control_valve_tamper.notes: Valve closed = system is impaired — must be corrected immediately
control_valve_tamper.critical: A closed sprinkler valve during a fire = catastrophic failure

low_pressure_switch.description: Monitors water pressure in sprinkler system
low_pressure_switch.notes: Low pressure may indicate water supply issue or leak

fire_pump_running.notes: Pump running when no flow = potential problem

interfaces.standards: NFPA 72 Section 21.5 / NFPA 13 / NFPA 20 / BS 5839-1 Section 21.4

interfaces.name: Access Control System Release
interfaces.trigger: General fire alarm signal (all zones alarm)

maglock_release.description: Release electromagnetic locks on egress doors
maglock_release.action: Remove power to maglocks to allow free egress
maglock_release.fail_safe: Must fail-safe — doors unlock on power loss

electric_strike_release.description: Release electric strikes on access-controlled doors
electric_strike_release.action: Energize strike to unlock (or de-energize if fail-safe strike)

interfaces.standards: NFPA 72 Section 21.12 / NFPA 101 (Life Safety Code) / BS 5839-1
interfaces.notes: ALL access-controlled egress doors must release on fire alarm — this is a life safety requirement, not optional

interfaces.name: Stairwell Pressurization

pressurization_fan_activation.description: Activate stairwell pressurization fans to keep stairwells smoke-free
pressurization_fan_activation.action: Start fans, open supply dampers, monitor differential pressure
pressurization_fan_activation.pressure_target: 25-50 Pa positive pressure in stairwell relative to adjacent spaces

pressure_monitoring.notes: May require dedicated pressure control system

interfaces.typical_use: High-rise buildings, underground buildings, shopping malls

interfaces.name: Emergency Lighting Activation

emergency_light_activation.description: Activate emergency lighting system (if not self-contained)
emergency_light_activation.action: Switch to battery-powered emergency lighting circuits

interfaces.notes: Many modern emergency lights are self-contained (battery within each fixture) and activate automatically on power failure — fire alarm interface may not be required

interfaces.name: Gas Supply Shut-Off
interfaces.trigger: Fire alarm signal in gas-equipped area
interfaces.typical_use: Commercial kitchens, boiler rooms, industrial gas systems

interfaces.name: Kitchen Hood Fire Suppression System

monitoring_points.system_off: SUPERVISORY — system is turned off or impaired

outputs.gas_shut_off: Close gas supply to cooking equipment
outputs.electrical_disconnect: Shut off power to cooking equipment below hood

interfaces.notes: Kitchen suppression systems are stand-alone but must be monitored by fire alarm

monitoring_points.pump_faul: Phase reversal, overtemperature, low oil, battery charger failure
monitoring_points.pump_controller_power: Loss of power to fire pump controller

interfaces.name: Smoke Curtain / Barrier Deployment

outputs.curtain_deploy: Deploy smoke curtain from ceiling-mounted housing
outputs.action: Motorized or gravity deployment — close opening to prevent smoke spread

interfaces.typical_use: Atriums, shopping malls, large open-plan buildings

sequence_of_operations_template.description: Template for documenting cause-and-effect sequences in design drawings

example.notes: All cars serving Floor 3 recalled to Ground Floor
